_purpose: Sample ARM template for AZ-104 exam interpretation exercises. The exam frequently shows ARM JSON snippets and asks what they deploy, what values are used, or what would change if a parameter is modified. Practice reading this top-to-bottom.

metadata.description: Base name for the storage account. ARM templates use 'parameters' to accept input values at deployment time — similar to Bicep 'param' declarations.
Azure region. The '[resourceGroup().location]' function returns the location of the target resource group — a very common default in ARM templates.

Storage redundancy SKU. 'allowedValues' restricts input to this list — the deployment fails if any other value is provided. AZ-104 tests knowledge of storage redundancy options: LRS (3 copies, 1 datacenter), ZRS (3 zones), GRS (6 copies, 2 regions), RA-GRS (GRS + read access to secondary).

variables._comment: Variables are computed once at deployment time. They cannot be overridden by the caller — only parameters can.
variables.storageAccountFullName_explanation: concat() joins strings. uniqueString() generates a deterministic 13-char hash from the resource group ID, ensuring global uniqueness. This is identical to Bicep's uniqueString() function.

resources._comment: Each resource block has: type, apiVersion, name, location, and resource-specific properties. On the AZ-104 exam, you may be asked to identify the resource type, understand what properties control, or predict what happens if a value changes.
resources.name: [variables('storageAccountFullName')]

properties._comment: StorageV2 (general-purpose v2) is the recommended kind for most scenarios. It supports blobs, files, queues, tables, and Data Lake Storage. AZ-104 tests the difference between StorageV1, StorageV2, BlobStorage, and BlockBlobStorage account kinds.

metadata.description: The primary blob endpoint URL. The reference() function retrieves runtime properties of a deployed resource. In Bicep, this is simply 'storageAccount.properties.primaryEndpoints.blob'.
Full resource ID. resourceId() constructs the ID from the resource type and name. AZ-104 may ask you to construct or interpret resource IDs.
